Example_Clause: This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless written notice is provided ninety days prior to expiration.
The contract will renew automatically unless cancelled in writing at least 120 days before the end of the term.
This is an evergreen agreement that continues indefinitely until terminated by either party with 180 days written notice.
Either party may elect not to renew this Agreement by providing written notice 30 days prior to expiration.
This Agreement may be renewed for one additional term upon mutual written agreement sixty days prior to expiration.
This Agreement shall renew only upon mutual written agreement of both parties prior to the expiration date.
The aggregate liability of Supplier shall not exceed the fees paid in the prior three months.
In no event shall Supplier liability exceed one month of average fees under this Agreement.
The total limitation of liability for either party shall be capped at fifty thousand dollars regardless of claim type.
Supplier liability shall not exceed the total fees paid in the prior twelve months under this Agreement.
Aggregate liability shall not exceed two times the annual contract value for direct damages only.
No limitation of liability shall apply in cases of gross negligence, willful misconduct, or breach of confidentiality obligations.
Supplier shall defend claims at its sole discretion and shall have no obligation to indemnify Customer for third party claims.
Customer shall indemnify and hold harmless Supplier against any and all claims arising from Customer use of services.
Customer shall defend against and bear all costs of any regulatory claims related to services provided.
Each party shall indemnify the other for claims arising from its own negligence or willful misconduct.
Both parties shall maintain adequate insurance and provide mutual indemnification for claims arising from their respective acts or omissions.
Supplier shall indemnify Customer for all claims arising from Supplier negligence, errors, or failure to meet agreed specifications.
All invoices are due and payable net 30 days from invoice date.
Payment is due upon receipt of invoice with no exceptions.
Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month compounded monthly from the due date.
Invoices shall be payable within sixty days of receipt by Customer accounts payable department.
All undisputed invoices shall be payable within seventy-five days of Customer receipt.
All undisputed invoices shall be payable within ninety days of Customer receipt in accordance with Customer standard payment terms.
Supplier reserves the right to adjust pricing annually with no cap on increases based on market conditions.
Pricing shall adjust annually based on the Consumer Price Index without limitation.
Supplier may implement a price adjustment at any time with thirty days written notice.
Annual price adjustments shall not exceed three percent per year based on documented CPI changes.
Pricing increases are limited to a maximum of two and a half percent annually with ninety days advance written notice.
Pricing shall remain fixed for the initial two-year term with any increases capped at two percent annually thereafter subject to mutual written agreement.
Early termination by Customer shall incur a termination fee equal to six months of average monthly fees.
Either party may terminate with one hundred eighty days written notice with no termination for cause provision.
Customer may not terminate this Agreement for convenience during the initial term under any circumstances.
Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon sixty days written notice without penalty.
Customer may terminate for convenience with ninety days written notice with no early termination penalties after year one.
Customer may terminate this Agreement immediately for cause including failure to meet SLAs, regulatory non-compliance, or insolvency of Supplier.
Neither party shall be liable for delays caused by circumstances beyond their reasonable control including supply chain disruptions.
Force majeure events including acts of God, pandemics, labor disputes, and government actions shall excuse performance indefinitely.
Supplier shall not be liable for any supply chain disruptions regardless of duration or impact on Customer operations.
In a force majeure event Supplier shall use reasonable efforts to resume performance within thirty days and notify Customer within 48 hours.
Force majeure events exceeding thirty days shall trigger a joint review to identify alternative solutions at no additional cost to Customer.
Force majeure events exceeding thirty days shall give Customer the right to terminate without penalty and immediately engage alternative suppliers.
All intellectual property developed under this Agreement shall remain the exclusive property of Supplier.
Work product shall not be considered work for hire and all rights remain with Supplier regardless of Customer funding.
Ownership of all process improvements and innovations developed during service delivery shall vest in Supplier.
Supplier grants Customer a non-exclusive license to use any work product developed specifically for Customer under this Agreement.
Jointly developed innovations shall be owned equally by both parties with neither party requiring consent for use.
All custom work product, process improvements, and innovations funded by Customer shall be owned exclusively by Customer upon payment.
Customer agrees that Supplier shall be the exclusive supplier of services for all Customer facilities during the term.
Customer designates Supplier as sole source provider and agrees not to engage alternative suppliers.
During the exclusivity period Customer shall direct one hundred percent of volume to Supplier.
Supplier shall be designated as preferred supplier with Customer retaining the right to dual-source for business continuity purposes.
Customer commits to a minimum annual volume with no exclusivity obligation and full rights to engage additional suppliers.
Nothing in this Agreement shall restrict Customer from engaging additional suppliers at its sole discretion at any time.
All disputes shall be resolved through binding arbitration and Customer expressly waives the right to jury trial.
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Ohio with exclusive jurisdiction in Franklin County courts.
The exclusive venue for all disputes shall be the courts of Supplier home state jurisdiction.
Disputes shall be resolved in the jurisdiction where the Customer facility involved in the dispute is located.
Parties agree to attempt mediation prior to initiating formal legal proceedings in any dispute.
Governing law and dispute resolution venue shall be mutually agreed upon with neither party having home jurisdiction advantage.
All dose mapping studies and revalidation activities shall be conducted at Supplier sole discretion and expense shall be borne by Customer.
Any revalidation required due to equipment changes shall be billed to Customer at Supplier standard rates with no advance notice requirement.
Supplier retains exclusive ownership of all dose mapping data and protocols developed under this Agreement.
Dose mapping studies required due to Supplier equipment changes shall be performed at Supplier cost with Customer approval of methodology.
Revalidation costs arising from regulatory changes shall be shared equally between Customer and Supplier.

All dose mapping protocols, revalidation activities, and methodology changes require prior written Customer approval and shall be performed at Supplier cost when driven by Supplier equipment or process changes.
Customer shall bear full responsibility and cost for any production delays resulting from bioburden excursions regardless of cause.
Supplier shall have no liability for sterilization failures resulting from Customer product bioburden levels exceeding historical averages.
Bioburden monitoring and control is the sole responsibility of Customer and Supplier assumes no liability for excursions.
Both parties shall maintain documented bioburden monitoring programs and share data quarterly to proactively manage sterilization efficacy.
Supplier shall notify Customer within 24 hours of any bioburden excursion with a written root cause analysis within five business days.
Supplier shall maintain validated bioburden monitoring procedures and bear responsibility for sterilization failures attributable to process deviations within Supplier control.
Sterilization certificates of conformance shall be provided within thirty business days of product release.
Sterilization documentation shall be provided at Supplier convenience with no guaranteed turnaround time.
Certificates of conformance are provided as a courtesy and Supplier makes no warranty regarding their accuracy or completeness.
Sterilization certificates of conformance shall be provided within five business days of product release.
All sterilization records and certificates shall be provided within three business days and retained by Supplier for a minimum of ten years.
Sterilization certificates of conformance shall be provided within 24 hours of product release and Supplier shall maintain complete sterilization records for the life of the product plus five years.
Supplier makes no guarantee of available sterilization capacity and Customer orders shall be fulfilled on a best-efforts basis.
Supplier provides no guarantee of processing turnaround times and shall not be liable for delays due to capacity constraints.
Customer orders shall be prioritized at Supplier sole discretion based on overall facility capacity utilization.
Supplier shall reserve sterilization capacity equivalent to Customer forecasted annual volume with ninety days advance notice of significant changes.
Supplier guarantees processing capacity for Customer baseline volume with a maximum turnaround time of five business days under normal conditions.

Supplier shall dedicate sterilization capacity to Customer based on annual volume forecast with guaranteed turnaround times and financial penalties for capacity failures exceeding agreed service levels.
Supplier maintains a single processing facility for Customer product with no documented backup site capability.
In the event of a facility disruption Supplier shall make best efforts to identify alternative processing options at Customer expense.
Customer acknowledges that Supplier operates from a sole facility and accepts the associated supply continuity risk.
Supplier maintains a documented business continuity plan with at least one qualified backup sterilization facility available within sixty days activation.
Supplier shall provide Customer with an annual review of its Business Continuity Plan and notify Customer of any material changes within thirty days.

Supplier shall maintain at minimum two qualified backup sterilization sites validated for Customer products and activate backup capacity within fifteen business days of a primary facility disruption at no additional cost to Customer.
Any costs associated with regulatory changes including FDA, EPA, or ISO standard updates shall be borne entirely by Customer.
Supplier shall pass through all regulatory compliance costs to Customer with thirty days written notice.
Changes required by government mandate shall be implemented at Customer expense regardless of which party triggers the requirement.
Regulatory change costs affecting the broader industry shall be shared equally between parties following mutual review and agreement.
Supplier shall provide ninety days advance notice of regulatory-driven price changes with supporting documentation for Customer budget planning purposes.
Regulatory changes required to maintain Supplier facility accreditation and licensure shall be borne entirely by Supplier as a cost of doing business.
Supplier reserves the right to modify sterilization processes and validation protocols at its sole discretion without Customer notification.
Process changes required by Supplier equipment upgrades shall be implemented without Customer approval and costs borne by Customer.
Customer waives the right to review or approve Supplier validation protocols provided final sterilization parameters are maintained.
Supplier shall provide sixty days prior notification of any process or validation protocol changes that may affect Customer product.
Material changes to sterilization validation protocols require Customer written approval prior to implementation with a minimum thirty day review period.
No changes to sterilization processes, validation protocols, equipment, or facility configurations that may affect Customer product shall be implemented without prior written Customer approval.
Responsibility for ETO residual testing and compliance with ISO 10993-7 limits rests entirely with Customer.
Supplier makes no guarantee regarding ETO residual levels and Customer bears all responsibility for product release decisions.
Aeration time requirements are provided as guidelines only and Customer assumes all risk for ETO residual compliance.
Supplier shall provide validated aeration parameters and documented ETO cycle data to support Customer residual testing and product release.
Supplier shall maintain validated ETO cycles that consistently achieve Customer-specified residual limits with documented cycle data provided for each lot.

Supplier warrants that validated ETO sterilization cycles shall consistently achieve residual levels compliant with ISO 10993-7 requirements with complete cycle documentation for every Customer lot processed.
Supplier may perform services at any of its facilities or those of its affiliates without prior notification to Customer.
Services may be provided by Supplier or its designated subsidiaries at Supplier sole discretion.
Supplier reserves the right to perform sterilization services at any of its facilities deemed appropriate by Supplier.
Supplier may subcontract or delegate performance of services to its agents or affiliates without Customer consent.
Services shall be performed at Supplier approved facilities with Customer notified of any facility changes within thirty days.

Services shall be performed exclusively at the following specifically named and approved facilities listed in Exhibit A, each identified by legal entity name and physical address. Any addition or removal of covered facilities requires prior written amendment executed by both parties.
This Agreement is entered into solely by the contracting entity and no parent company or affiliate assumes any obligation hereunder.
Each Supplier facility operates as a separate legal entity and the obligations of one entity shall not be imputed to any other entity within the corporate family.
Customer's rights and remedies under this Agreement are limited solely to the contracting entity and shall not extend to any parent, affiliate, or subsidiary.
Parent company acknowledges this Agreement and agrees to provide operational support to the contracting subsidiary to ensure continuity of services.
Parent company provides a limited guarantee of the contracting subsidiary's performance obligations for the initial term of this Agreement.

The ultimate parent company unconditionally and irrevocably guarantees all performance obligations of the contracting entity under this Agreement. In the event of dissolution, bankruptcy, insolvency, regulatory shutdown, or cessation of operations by the contracting entity, parent company shall immediately assume all obligations and ensure uninterrupted service continuity at no additional cost to Customer.
Supplier may relocate, consolidate, or change the facilities used to process Customer products at its sole discretion without Customer approval.
Supplier reserves the right to transfer Customer product processing to alternative facilities without prior notice to Customer.
Facility assignments may change based on Supplier operational requirements and capacity constraints without Customer consent.
Supplier shall provide Customer with thirty days written notice prior to any change in the facility used to process Customer products.
Any change to the approved facility or legal entity performing services requires prior written notification to Customer with a minimum sixty day transition period.

No change to the approved processing facility or contracting legal entity shall be made without prior written Customer approval and formal amendment to this Agreement. All replacement facilities must be pre-qualified and approved by Customer Quality prior to any product transfer.
Services are provided by Supplier LLC, a separately incorporated entity, and the obligations of Supplier LLC do not extend to any related entities within the corporate family.
The limited liability structure of the contracting entity limits Customer's recourse to the assets of that specific entity only.
Each facility is separately incorporated and operates independently with no cross-liability between entities.
Notwithstanding the LLC structure of individual facilities, the corporate parent provides performance assurance for all contracted services.
A material default by any facility entity under this Agreement shall constitute a default by all related entities and the parent company.

Customer acknowledges that individual facilities may operate as separate LLC entities. Supplier warrants that the ultimate parent company provides full unconditional backstop for all performance obligations regardless of which entity performs the services. A schedule of all facility LLCs covered under this Agreement is attached as Exhibit A and shall be kept current by Supplier.
Each facility maintains its own separate insurance coverage and claims must be filed against the specific facility entity where the loss occurred.
Insurance coverage is limited to the per occurrence limits maintained by the specific contracting entity and does not aggregate across related entities.
Each LLC maintains a separate insurance policy and the aggregate limits of one entity's policy shall not be available to satisfy claims arising at another entity.
Each facility entity shall maintain minimum insurance coverage of ten million dollars per occurrence for product liability and sterilization errors and omissions.
Parent company maintains an umbrella insurance policy that provides additional coverage above individual facility policy limits for claims arising under this Agreement.

Supplier shall maintain consolidated enterprise-wide insurance coverage naming Customer as additional insured with minimum limits of twenty-five million dollars per occurrence and fifty million dollars aggregate across all covered facilities. Evidence of coverage shall be provided annually and upon Customer request.
Regulatory actions issued against a specific facility entity shall not constitute a breach of this Agreement by the Supplier parent or other facility entities.
A consent decree or warning letter issued against one Supplier facility shall not affect the obligations of other Supplier entities under this Agreement.
Regulatory findings at one facility entity are not attributable to related entities and shall not trigger termination rights under this Agreement.
Customer may suspend shipments to any facility subject to a regulatory action that materially impacts that facility's ability to perform validated sterilization services.

Supplier shall notify Customer within 24 hours of any regulatory action including FDA warning letters, consent decrees, or facility shutdowns affecting any entity performing services under this Agreement.

Any regulatory action including FDA warning letters, consent decrees, import alerts, or facility shutdowns issued against any entity performing services under this Agreement shall give Customer the immediate right to terminate the affected facility's obligations, transfer product to an approved alternative facility at Supplier cost, and if no qualified alternative exists, terminate this Agreement in its entirety without penalty.
This Agreement is entered into by and between Buyer and Supplier as represented by their authorized representatives only.
Risk_Explanation: Missing parent company, LLC entity names, or authorized signatory — contract may not bind the correct legal entity.

Example_Clause: This Agreement is entered into between Buyer Corporation and Supplier LLC, including all subsidiaries and affiliated entities operating under the same ultimate parent.
Risk_Explanation: Clear identification of all contracting entities including subsidiaries.

Example_Clause: Capitalized terms used in this Agreement shall have the meanings assigned to them herein without further definition in applicable sections.
Risk_Explanation: Vague definitions allow supplier to reinterpret key terms — especially dangerous for 'Specifications', 'Defect', and 'Delivery'.

Example_Clause: 'Product' shall mean the items listed in Exhibit A as amended from time to time by mutual written agreement. 'Defect' shall mean any nonconformance with the Specifications.
Risk_Explanation: Clear definitions reduce ambiguity and dispute risk.

Example_Clause: This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless written notice is provided ninety days prior to expiration.
Risk_Explanation: Auto-renewal with 90-day notice window creates trapped spend — favors supplier.

Example_Clause: This Agreement has an initial term of three years commencing on the Effective Date. Either party may elect not to renew by providing 30 days written notice prior to expiration.
Risk_Explanation: Defined term with short notice period preserves buyer flexibility.

Example_Clause: The scope of products to be supplied shall be as mutually agreed between the parties from time to time.
Risk_Explanation: Undefined scope gives supplier leverage to add products or change terms without formal amendment.

Example_Clause: The Products to be supplied are set forth in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated by reference. Changes to scope require written amendment signed by both parties.
Risk_Explanation: Defined scope in exhibit protects buyer from unauthorized changes.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall provide Products meeting generally accepted industry standards for quality and performance.
Risk_Explanation: 'Generally accepted' is unenforceable — no specific standard to hold supplier to.

Example_Clause: Products shall conform to the Specifications attached as Exhibit B. Supplier shall not deviate from the Specifications without prior written approval from Buyer.
Risk_Explanation: Specific attached specifications with change control protection.

Example_Clause: Buyer shall provide Supplier with best efforts volume forecasts on a quarterly basis for planning purposes only.
Risk_Explanation: 'Planning purposes only' forecasts give supplier no obligation to hold capacity — creates supply risk.

Example_Clause: Buyer shall provide a rolling 12-month forecast updated monthly. The first three months shall be binding purchase commitments. Supplier shall maintain capacity to fulfill 120% of the binding forecast.
Risk_Explanation: Binding near-term forecast with capacity buffer protects supply continuity.

Example_Clause: Pricing is subject to change with thirty days notice based on raw material cost fluctuations and market conditions.
Risk_Explanation: Unilateral pricing change right with only 30-day notice exposes buyer to cost volatility.

Example_Clause: Prices are fixed for the initial contract term. Any price adjustments require written agreement and shall not exceed the published CPI index for the relevant period.
Risk_Explanation: Price stability with CPI-indexed cap protects buyer budget.

Example_Clause: All deliveries shall be made Ex Works (EXW) Supplier's facility. Risk of loss and title transfer at Supplier's loading dock.
Risk_Explanation: EXW puts freight risk and cost entirely on buyer — unfavorable for regulated goods.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall deliver Products DDP (Delivered Duty Paid) to Buyer's designated facility. Supplier bears all freight, insurance, and customs costs and risk of loss until receipt at Buyer's dock.
Risk_Explanation: DDP maximizes buyer protection — all delivery risk stays with supplier.

Example_Clause: Title and risk of loss pass to Buyer FOB Origin at Supplier's shipping dock.
Risk_Explanation: FOB Origin means buyer assumes risk during transit — especially problematic for regulated goods.

Example_Clause: Title and risk of loss shall pass to Buyer upon delivery to and acceptance at Buyer's designated facility (FOB Destination).
Risk_Explanation: FOB Destination keeps risk with supplier until confirmed receipt and acceptance.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall package Products in standard commercial packaging suitable for shipping.
Risk_Explanation: 'Standard commercial' packaging may not meet regulatory or cleanroom requirements for medical devices.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall package and label Products in compliance with all applicable regulatory requirements including FDA, ISO 11607, and any Buyer-specific requirements set forth in the Specifications.
Risk_Explanation: Explicit regulatory packaging compliance requirement.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall invoice Buyer upon shipment. Payment is due within the terms stated regardless of receipt or acceptance status.
Risk_Explanation: Payment triggered by shipment not acceptance — buyer may pay for goods not yet received or accepted.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall invoice Buyer upon Buyer's written acceptance of the Products. Payment terms commence from the date of accepted invoice.
Risk_Explanation: Payment clock starts only after acceptance — protects buyer cash flow and quality leverage.

Example_Clause: All prices are exclusive of taxes. Buyer shall pay all applicable taxes, duties, levies, and customs charges in addition to the stated price.
Risk_Explanation: Broad tax pass-through with no cap — buyer exposed to unknown customs and import duty costs.

Example_Clause: Prices stated are inclusive of all applicable taxes except for taxes based on Buyer's net income. Supplier is responsible for all export duties and origin-country taxes.
Risk_Explanation: Clear tax allocation — supplier bears origin-country costs.

Example_Clause: Prices are stated in USD and subject to exchange rate adjustment if currency fluctuations exceed 5% from the contract date rate.
Risk_Explanation: Currency adjustment clause exposes buyer to FX risk on international supply.

Example_Clause: All prices and payments under this Agreement shall be in United States Dollars. Exchange rate fluctuations shall not affect the stated contract price.
Risk_Explanation: Fixed USD pricing eliminates FX exposure for buyer.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall provide Products of commercially reasonable quality consistent with industry norms.
Risk_Explanation: 'Commercially reasonable' is subjective and unenforceable in regulated manufacturing environments.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall maintain a quality management system certified to ISO 13485 and shall provide Products meeting the Specifications and all applicable regulatory requirements.
Risk_Explanation: Specific quality system certification requirement with regulatory alignment.

Example_Clause: Products not rejected within five business days of delivery shall be deemed accepted by Buyer.
Risk_Explanation: Short deemed acceptance window — buyer may be stuck with defective goods if inspection takes longer.

Example_Clause: Buyer shall have 30 days following receipt to inspect Products. Payment of an invoice shall not constitute acceptance. Buyer may reject nonconforming Products at any time within the warranty period.
Risk_Explanation: Extended inspection window with right to reject throughout warranty period.

Example_Clause: SUPPLIER MAKES NO WARRANTIES EXPRESS OR IMPLIED INCLUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
Risk_Explanation: Complete warranty disclaimer — buyer has no recourse for defective products.

Example_Clause: Supplier warrants that Products will conform to Specifications, be free from defects in materials and workmanship, and comply with all applicable laws for a period of 24 months from delivery.
Risk_Explanation: Strong express warranty with defined term and scope.

Example_Clause: Buyer's sole and exclusive remedy for nonconforming Products shall be replacement of the defective Products at Supplier's option.
Risk_Explanation: Limiting buyer to replacement only — no right to refund, cover, or consequential damages.

Example_Clause: If Products fail to conform to Specifications, Buyer may at its option require Supplier to repair, replace, or provide a full refund. Supplier shall also reimburse Buyer for reasonable cover costs if Supplier cannot supply conforming goods within 30 days.
Risk_Explanation: Buyer retains choice of remedy including refund and cover reimbursement.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall comply with applicable laws to the extent required by applicable regulations.
Risk_Explanation: 'To the extent required' is self-limiting — supplier can argue partial compliance.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall comply with all applicable federal, state, local, and international laws, regulations, and standards in the performance of this Agreement including but not limited to FDA, EPA, OSHA, and applicable import/export laws.
Risk_Explanation: Comprehensive compliance obligation with specific regulatory bodies named.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall comply with regulatory requirements as applicable to the Products.
Risk_Explanation: Vague regulatory obligation — supplier can argue specific requirements don't apply.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall comply with all applicable FDA regulations including 21 CFR Parts 820 and 211, ISO 11135/11137, and shall maintain all required registrations and licenses. Supplier shall notify Buyer within 24 hours of any regulatory action, inspection, or citation.
Risk_Explanation: Specific regulatory standards cited with immediate notification requirement.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall use best efforts to conduct business in an ethical manner consistent with general business norms.
Risk_Explanation: 'Best efforts' and 'general norms' are unenforceable — no specific standards.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall comply with Buyer's Supplier Code of Conduct as updated from time to time, prohibiting forced labor, child labor, bribery, corruption, and environmental violations. Supplier shall cascade these requirements to all sub-tier suppliers.
Risk_Explanation: Specific code compliance with sub-tier cascade requirement.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall maintain adequate insurance coverage as required by applicable law.
Risk_Explanation: 'Adequate' is undefined — supplier may carry insufficient coverage for regulated manufacturing exposure.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall maintain at minimum: (i) Commercial General Liability of $5M per occurrence; (ii) Product Liability of $10M per occurrence; (iii) Workers Compensation as required by law; (iv) Professional Liability of $2M. Buyer shall be named as additional insured on all policies.
Risk_Explanation: Specific coverage minimums with buyer named as additional insured.

Example_Clause: In the event of a product recall, costs of the recall shall be shared equally between Buyer and Supplier regardless of the cause.
Risk_Explanation: 50/50 cost sharing even when recall is supplier's fault — unfair to buyer.

Example_Clause: If a recall is required due to Supplier's failure to meet Specifications or comply with applicable law, Supplier shall bear all recall costs including logistics, customer notification, and replacement. Supplier shall cooperate fully with regulatory agencies and Buyer in all recall activities.
Risk_Explanation: Supplier bears full cost when recall is caused by supplier failure.

Example_Clause: Each party shall use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the other party's confidential information.
Risk_Explanation: 'Reasonable efforts' is a weak standard — should be 'same degree of care as own confidential information'.

Example_Clause: Each party shall protect Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care. Confidential Information shall not be disclosed for five years following termination.
Risk_Explanation: Strong confidentiality standard with post-termination survival clause.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall protect personal data using industry standard security measures.
Risk_Explanation: 'Industry standard' is undefined and may not meet GDPR, HIPAA, or CCPA requirements.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall process personal data only as instructed by Buyer and in compliance with applicable data protection laws including GDPR and HIPAA. Supplier shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures and notify Buyer within 24 hours of any data breach.
Risk_Explanation: Specific regulatory compliance with 24-hour breach notification.

Example_Clause: Neither party may terminate this Agreement for convenience during the initial term. Termination prior to expiration requires mutual written agreement.
Risk_Explanation: No exit right during term — buyer is locked in even if supplier performance deteriorates.

Example_Clause: Buyer may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon 90 days written notice to Supplier. Supplier shall fulfill all open purchase orders accepted prior to notice of termination.
Risk_Explanation: Buyer exit right with reasonable notice period — maintains optionality.

Example_Clause: Either party may terminate for cause upon 30 days written notice if the other party has materially breached this Agreement and failed to cure within said 30-day period.
Risk_Explanation: 30-day cure period may be too long for quality or supply failures in regulated manufacturing.

Example_Clause: Buyer may terminate immediately upon written notice if Supplier: (i) fails to meet quality Specifications on three or more occasions in any 12-month period; (ii) is subject to FDA enforcement action; (iii) becomes insolvent; or (iv) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure within 15 days of written notice.
Risk_Explanation: Immediate termination triggers for quality, regulatory, and financial failures.

Example_Clause: Upon termination, all obligations of the parties shall cease and neither party shall have any further liability to the other.
Risk_Explanation: Blanket release on termination — eliminates warranty, indemnity, and data return obligations.

Example_Clause: Upon termination: (i) Supplier shall fulfill all open accepted orders; (ii) all payment obligations survive; (iii) confidentiality, IP, indemnification, and warranty obligations survive for their stated terms; (iv) Supplier shall return or destroy all Buyer confidential information within 30 days.
Risk_Explanation: Comprehensive survival clause protecting buyer's post-termination interests.

Example_Clause: Any disputes arising under this Agreement shall be resolved in the courts of the jurisdiction where Supplier is incorporated.
Risk_Explanation: Supplier's home jurisdiction gives supplier advantage in litigation — unfavorable for buyer.

Example_Clause: Any dispute arising under this Agreement shall first be subject to good-faith negotiation for 30 days. Unresolved disputes shall be submitted to binding arbitration under AAA Commercial Rules in a mutually agreed neutral jurisdiction.
Risk_Explanation: Neutral arbitration avoids home-court advantage for either party.

Example_Clause: Supplier may assign this Agreement or any of its rights and obligations hereunder without Buyer's prior written consent.
Risk_Explanation: Supplier can assign contract to unknown third party — Buyer may end up with a different supplier.

Example_Clause: Neither party may assign this Agreement without prior written consent of the other party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Any assignment without consent is void.
Risk_Explanation: Mutual consent requirement prevents unwanted supplier substitution.

Example_Clause: Supplier may subcontract any portion of its obligations under this Agreement to qualified third parties at Supplier's discretion.
Risk_Explanation: Unlimited subcontracting right — supplier can outsource critical work without buyer knowledge.

Example_Clause: Supplier shall not subcontract any manufacturing, sterilization, or quality functions without Buyer's prior written approval. Supplier remains fully liable for subcontractor performance. Supplier shall flow down all quality and compliance requirements to approved subcontractors.
Risk_Explanation: Approval requirement with liability retention and flow-down obligations.

Example_Clause: This Agreement may be amended by Supplier upon 30 days written notice to Buyer.
Risk_Explanation: Unilateral amendment right — Supplier can change terms without Buyer agreement.

Example_Clause: This Agreement may only be amended by a written instrument signed by authorized representatives of both parties. No oral modifications shall be binding.
Risk_Explanation: Mutual consent requirement prevents unilateral changes.

Example_Clause: Notices may be provided by email to the last known email address of the receiving party.
Risk_Explanation: Email-only notice is insufficient for formal legal notices — delivery cannot be confirmed.

Example_Clause: All formal notices shall be in writing and delivered by: (i) certified mail return receipt requested; (ii) overnight courier with confirmation; or (iii) email with read receipt to the addresses stated in the Agreement header.
Risk_Explanation: Multiple notice methods with confirmation — ensures delivery can be proven.

Example_Clause: In the event of conflict between this Agreement and any purchase order, the terms of the purchase order shall govern.
Risk_Explanation: PO governs over master agreement — supplier can override negotiated terms with simple PO terms.

Example_Clause: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations. In the event of conflict between this Agreement and any purchase order, this Agreement shall govern unless the purchase order specifically references and amends this Agreement.
Risk_Explanation: Master agreement prevails — protects negotiated terms from PO override.
